channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: YouTube is cracking down on gambling content. Here’s what’s changing
item.description: YouTube is taking steps to crack down on gambling content. On Tuesday, the platform announced a new policy that bans creators from directing viewers to “unapproved” gambling websites through links, images, text, logos, or verbal mentions.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX’s Starship explodes again, with wreckage seen from Florida
item.description: Nearly two months after an explosion sent flaming debris raining down on the Turks and Caicos, SpaceX launched another mammoth Starship rocket on Thursday, but lost contact minutes into the test flight as the spacecraft came tumbling down and broke apart.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Scientists date remains of an ancient child that resembles both humans and Neanderthals
item.description: Scientists have dated the skeleton of an ancient child that caused a stir when it was first discovered because it carries features from both humans and Neanderthals.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: MikroTik's ROSE Data Server RDS2216 Combines Storage, Networking, and Containers
item.description: MikroTik's new enterprise server, powered by ROSE, combines storage, networking, and containers for enterprise environments.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride
item.description: This blog will focus on requirements in the context of the federal government. Sound requirements drive best value pricing and performance for customer agencies The post Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Out of the lab and into the streets, researchers and doctors rally for science against Trump cuts
item.description: Researchers, doctors, their patients and supporters are venturing out of labs, hospitals and offices across the country to stand up to what they call an attack on life-saving science by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US military's mini space shuttle returns to Earth after orbiting for 434 days on a secret mission
item.description: The U.S. military's classified mini space shuttle has returned to Earth after circling the world for 434 days.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Disco Elysium successor XXX Nightshift nets some serious musical talent
item.description: Disco Elysium successor XXX Nightshift will feature a soundtrack led by songwriter and music producer John Cunningham.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: AMD might go for Nvidia’s jugular in Q2 with a faster RX 9070 ‘Extreme’ GPU that could leave the RTX 5070 Ti in the dust
item.description: If AMD has Nvidia on the ropes now, rumored RX 9070 XTX model that’s faster than the RTX 5070 Ti could be a knockout blow.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Walters: California’s list of failed tech projects just keeps growing
item.description: The State Bar Exam chaos is yet another expensive and troubled effort to incorporate cutting-edge technology.
item.dc:creator: Dan Walters, The Mercury News

item.title: Android 16 beta users are reporting major battery drain issues – but I’m not too worried about it
item.description: The latest Android 16 beta seems to be putting increased stress on phones’ battery life, with some users reporting the need to charge up twice a day – but with a stable release still a ways off, there’s time for Google to fix it.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Anthropic’s upgraded Console targets more collaboration among developers
item.description: Anthropic has upgraded its Console — an interface that lets enterprises create API keys, add more users to a team, set up billing, and experiment with Claude on the Workbench, in an effort to promote more collaboration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: ExpressVPN releases a major upgrade to its Linux app
item.description: The ExpressVPN Linux app now includes the much requested GUI, configurable kill switch, and split-tunneling, promising enhanced security and a user-centric experience.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Google Chrome launches better warning labels to make sure you know you're using a company profile
item.description: Google doesn’t want you mixing personal and work browsing sessions with its latest Chrome Enterprise update.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Stop carrying your physical driver's license and add it to your Apple Wallet
item.description: A digital ID may be even more secure than a physical ID. Storing your driver's license in your Apple Wallet is an ideal solution for commuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Davos 2025: Key priorities and truths for CIOs
item.description: Davos 2025 was a blend of optimism and urgency. One data point presented at the event showed that 62% of business leaders from large organizations expressed confidence in their growth prospects (Capgemini Research Institute).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This compact new headphone amp from a hi-fi legend will boost sound quality for wired or wireless headphones
item.description: Rotel's DX-3 promises exceptional high-end audio for elite headphones, even if you're using aptX Bluetooth.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Don’t panic, gaming laptop buyers – Nvidia assures us that mobile RTX 5000 graphics cards won’t have the chip-level fault that hit desktop GPUs
item.description: The (rare) problem with missing rendering pipelines only applies to desktop graphics cards, not the laptop variety for RTX 5000.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Taiwan says chipmaker’s move to invest $100 billion in the US wasn’t because of US pressure
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan’s president and top chipmaker TSMC held a joint news conference Thursday to defend the company’s decision to invest $100 billion in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI chatbots infected with Russian disinformation: Study
item.description: The world’s most popular artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots are infected with Russian disinformation, according to a study published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation
item.description: All components of a zero trust architecture must generate data that is observable, available and able to be correlated within a unified data plane. The post Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Chrome Enterprise moves to better differentiate work, personal browsing
item.description: Google on Thursday unveiled new security controls for Chrome Enterprise, allowing IT departments to help employees better distinguish between work and personal browsing profiles.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Democrats ask for investigation into whether Musk is using government position to influence X advertisers
item.description: Democratic senators are asking the Department of Justice (DOJ) to investigate whether tech billionaire Elon Musk, a senior adviser to President Trump, is leveraging his government position to pressure advertisers into spending more on his social media platform, X.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: LibreOffice downloads on the rise as users look to avoid subscription costs
item.description: Interest in LibreOffice, the open-source alternative to Microsoft Office, is on the rise, with weekly downloads of its software package close to 1 million a week. That’s the highest download number since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump calls CHIPS Act ‘horrible,’ wants to defund it
item.description: In his address to Congress last night, President Donald J. Trump called the CHIPS Act “horrible,” and said whatever money hasn’t been spent should now be used to reduce the national debt. The CHIPS Act, enacted into law in 2022 under then-President Joseph R. Biden Jr.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5
